desc: Take a /etc/passwd-formatted string in arg0 (lines of colon-separated fields). Print just the LAST field (login shell) of each line, one per line.
Take a CSV string in arg0 (no embedded commas, comma-separated). Print just the 3rd field (1-indexed) of each line.
Take a multi-line string in arg0 and a prefix in arg1. Print lines that start with the prefix, in original order.
args:
apple pie
banana split
apricot jam
cherry tart
apple cake
expected:
apple pie
apricot jam
apple cake

desc: Take a multi-line string in arg0 and an integer N in arg1. Print lines whose length is strictly greater than N.
args:
short
this is a longer line
hi
medium length
x
expected:
this is a longer line
medium length

desc: Take a multi-line string in arg0. Print the lines sorted alphabetically.
python:
import sys
for l in sorted(sys.argv[1].split('\n')): print(l)

desc: Take a string with whitespace-separated integers in arg0. Print them sorted in descending order, one per line.
python:
import sys
for n in sorted([int(x) for x in sys.argv[1].split()], reverse=True): print(n)

desc: Take a string in arg0. Print the count of distinct whitespace-separated tokens.
args: the quick brown fox the lazy dog the fox
python:
import sys
print(len(set(sys.argv[1].split())))

desc: Take a multi-line string in arg0 and two 1-indexed integers M and N in arg1 and arg2. Print lines M through N inclusive.
python:
import sys
lines = sys.argv[1].split('\n')
m, n = int(sys.argv[2]), int(sys.argv[3])
for l in lines[m-1:n]: print(l)

desc: Take a string in arg0. Print its character count (length in characters, not bytes).
python:
import sys
print(len(sys.argv[1]))

desc: Take a multi-line string in arg0. Print the count of lines that are not empty (have at least one character).
python:
import sys
print(sum(1 for l in sys.argv[1].split('\n') if l))

desc: Take a string in arg0 with one filesystem path per line. Print the basename (last component after final /) of each, one per line.
Take a tab-separated multi-line string in arg0. Print the sum of integers in the 2nd column.
python:
import sys
print(sum(int(l.split('\t')[1]) for l in sys.argv[1].split('\n')))

desc: Take a string in arg0. Print it with the first letter of each whitespace-separated word capitalized, the rest lowercase.
python:
import sys
print(' '.join(w.capitalize() for w in sys.argv[1].split()))

desc: Take a multi-line string in arg0. Print all non-blank lines (in original order, no blank lines in output).
Take a string in arg0. Apply ROT13 transformation to ASCII letters (a-z and A-Z); leave other characters unchanged.
python:
import sys, codecs
print(codecs.encode(sys.argv[1], 'rot_13'))

desc: Take a multi-line log string in arg0 where each line starts with one of INFO, WARN, ERROR (followed by space and message). Print 'LEVEL=COUNT' lines for each LEVEL that appears, sorted alphabetically by LEVEL.
args:
INFO server started
ERROR connection refused
INFO request received
WARN slow query
ERROR timeout
INFO request done
python:
import sys
from collections import Counter
c = Counter(l.split(' ', 1)[0] for l in sys.argv[1].split('\n'))
for k in sorted(c): print(f'{k}={c[k]}')

desc: Take a multi-line string in arg0 containing access-log-style entries. Each line has a 3-digit HTTP status code surrounded by spaces. Print the status codes in order of appearance, one per line.
Take a string in arg0. Extract all 10-digit phone numbers in the format NNN-NNN-NNNN (digits and hyphens, exactly that shape). Print each on its own line in order of appearance.
Take a string in arg0. Extract all hex color codes in the format #RRGGBB (a # followed by 6 hex digits). Print each on its own line in order of appearance, lowercase.
args: color: #FF0000; bg: #00ff00; border: #0000FF; small: #abc; mid: #DEAD42 here

desc: Take a JSON string in arg0 of the form {"items":[...]}. Print the count of items in the array.
python:
import sys, json
print(len(json.loads(sys.argv[1])['items']))

desc: Take a tab-separated multi-line string in arg0 (no commas in fields). Print the same data with tabs converted to commas.
args:
name	age	city
alice	30	paris
bob	25	nyc
python:
import sys
print(sys.argv[1].replace('\t', ','))

desc: Take a multi-line string in arg0 (each line a value). Print all lines joined by commas on a single line, followed by a newline.
python:
import sys
print(','.join(sys.argv[1].split('\n')))

desc: Take a string in arg0. Print 'yes' if every opening parenthesis has a matching closing one (and they nest correctly using only '(' and ')'); print 'no' otherwise.
Take a string in arg0. Print the distinct characters that appear in it, sorted by ASCII code, all on one line with no separator.
python:
import sys
print(''.join(sorted(set(sys.argv[1]))))

desc: Take a multi-line string in arg0. Print the same content with all letters uppercased.
python:
import sys
print(sys.argv[1].upper())
